req.title: Schedule performance review — variant 2
req.statement: A manager shall schedule a performance review with the reviewee at least 7 days in advance; past or sub-7-day dates shall be rejected. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines three key partitions: (1) dates >= now+7d are valid, (2) dates < now+7d are invalid (422), and (3) the exact boundary at now+7d is the critical edge. A fourth test covers idempotency under retry (STC: scheduled -> retry -> same state). EP covers the happy-path valid date well inside the window. BVA covers exactly +7d (inclusive lower bound of valid range) and exactly +6d 23h 59m 59s (exclusive upper bound of invalid range). NEG covers a past date. STC covers the idempotency retry requirement. DT would be redundant given the single boolean condition, so STC is substituted for the retry variant as it tests a state-machine property. All tests are self-contained via fixtures and helpers, deterministic via frozen-time offsets, and idempotent via unique review IDs.

tests.name: test_schedule_review_valid_date_far_future
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role:manager
A valid reviewee employee ID exists in the system
The target date is now + 14 days (well inside the valid partition)
expected: Notification events are queued for manager and reviewee
trace: R-WEB-093: valid date >= now+7d shall be accepted
R-WEB-093: participants shall be notified on success
tests.executable:
def test_schedule_review_valid_date_far_future(manager_session, reviewee_id, api_base_url):
    scheduled_at = <helper_fn.future_iso(days=14)>
    payload = {"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at}
    resp = manager_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "review_id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "scheduled"
    notifs = <helper_fn.get_pending_notifications(body["review_id"])>
    assert len(notifs) >= 2

tests.name: test_schedule_review_past_date_rejected
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role:manager
A valid reviewee employee ID exists in the system
The target date is yesterday (clearly in the past)
expected: Response body contains error code indicating date constraint violation
No review record is created in the database
trace: R-WEB-093: past dates shall be rejected with 422
R-WEB-093: scheduled_at < now+7d triggers error pathway
tests.executable:
def test_schedule_review_past_date_rejected(manager_session, reviewee_id, api_base_url):
    scheduled_at = <helper_fn.past_iso(days=1)>
    payload = {"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at}
    resp = manager_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "scheduled_at" in str(body).lower() or "date" in str(body).lower()
    reviews = <helper_fn.get_reviews_by_reviewee(reviewee_id)>
    assert all(r["scheduled_at"] != scheduled_at for r in reviews)

tests.name: test_schedule_review_exactly_7_days_boundary_accepted
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role:manager
A valid reviewee employee ID exists in the system
The target date is exactly now + 7 days (lower inclusive boundary of valid partition)
actions: POST /performance-reviews/schedule with scheduled_at = now + exactly 7 days (same hour/minute)
trace: R-WEB-093: at least 7 days in advance means +7d is the inclusive lower bound
R-WEB-093: BVA on the valid/invalid boundary
tests.executable:
def test_schedule_review_exactly_7_days_boundary_accepted(manager_session, reviewee_id, api_base_url):
    scheduled_at = <helper_fn.future_iso(days=7)>
    payload = {"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at}
    resp = manager_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "scheduled"

tests.name: test_schedule_review_6_days_23h_boundary_rejected
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role:manager
A valid reviewee employee ID exists in the system
The target date is now + 7 days minus 1 second (upper boundary of invalid partition)
actions: POST /performance-reviews/schedule with scheduled_at = now + 7 days - 1 second
expected: Response body signals date is too soon
trace: R-WEB-093: sub-7-day dates shall be rejected
R-WEB-093: BVA on the invalid side of the 7-day boundary

tests.name: test_schedule_review_idempotent_under_retry
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role:manager
A valid reviewee employee ID exists in the system
An idempotency key is generated for the request
The target date is now + 14 days
actions: POST /performance-reviews/schedule with idempotency_key header — first call
POST /performance-reviews/schedule with the same idempotency_key header — second call (retry)
expected: Both responses contain the same review_id
Only one review record exists in the system
trace: R-WEB-093: the action must be idempotent under retry
STC: state transitions scheduled->retry->same scheduled state without side effects
tests.executable:
def test_schedule_review_idempotent_under_retry(manager_session, reviewee_id, api_base_url):
    scheduled_at = <helper_fn.future_iso(days=14)>
    idem_key = <helper_fn.unique_key()>
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idem_key}
    payload = {"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at}
    resp1 = manager_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload, headers=headers)
    resp2 = manager_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp1.status_code == 201
    assert resp2.status_code == 201
    assert resp1.json()["review_id"] == resp2.json()["review_id"]
    records = <helper_fn.get_reviews_by_idempotency_key(idem_key)>
    assert len(records) == 1
    notifs = <helper_fn.get_pending_notifications(resp1.json()["review_id"])>
    assert len(notifs) == 2
